_comment: Checkpoint templates by scenario type. Loaded at runtime by the dispatcher (DK-705). No TypeScript changes needed to adjust timing, descriptions, or add new scenario types. See docs/sprints/sprint-7-plan.md §Configuration.
_revision: 2026-06-12 — initial Sprint 7 templates. Domain Expert interviews may refine timing and task descriptions.

EXPEDITE._comment: Full air/expedite freight. Three checkpoints: operational arrival confirmation (short window), financial cost reconciliation (medium), business impact on customer commitments (long).

checkpoints.task_description: Confirm whether the expedited shipment arrived on schedule and whether production was able to continue without interruption.
Confirm the actual expedite cost and whether it differed from the estimate shown at decision time. Include any additional handling fees.
Confirm whether any customer commitments or delivery promises were affected by the original delay or the expedite decision.

WAIT._comment: Standard wait — accept the delay. Operational confirmation tight (vessel arrival), business impact broader window.

checkpoints.task_description: Confirm the actual arrival date of the shipment and whether production was interrupted or delayed during the wait period.
Confirm whether any customer commitments were at risk or missed as a result of the delayed shipment.

PARTIAL_EXPEDITE._comment: Partial expedite — expedite critical components only. Operational checkpoint covers partial arrival; financial covers split cost.

checkpoints.task_description: Confirm which components were expedited, when they arrived, and whether the partial delivery was sufficient to prevent a production stoppage.
Confirm the actual cost breakdown between the expedited and standard delivery legs, and compare to the estimate shown at decision time.

REROUTE._comment: Alternative route. Operational confirmation covers new route delivery; financial covers cost delta vs original routing.

checkpoints.task_description: Confirm the actual arrival date via the alternative route and whether the rerouting prevented or reduced the production disruption.
Confirm the actual cost of the rerouting decision versus the baseline cost estimate. Include any additional customs or port fees.

_fallback._comment: Used when scenario_chosen has no explicit template. Single operational checkpoint. Edit this to change the default for any unrecognised scenario type.

checkpoints.task_description: Confirm what happened operationally after this decision was implemented. Include actual arrival date and any production impact.
